DECLARATION OF CHERYL L GUSTAFSON IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S OPPOS. TO DEFT'S FMTN TO TRANSFER VENUE TO SACRAMENTO FILED BY PLAINTIFF HATLEY, MARGARET , AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CHARLES SCHWAB & CO., INC'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY IS GRANTED BASED ON GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 12965 (B). THE PARTIES AGREE THAT SECTION 12965 (B) IS THE CONTROLLING VENUE STATUTE. THE DECLARATIONS SUBMITTED BY THE DEFENDANTS DEMONSTRATE THAT SACRAMENTO COUNTY IS THE PROPER VENUE FOR THIS DISPUTE. THE DECISION TO TERMINATE PLAINTIFF OCCURRED THERE, THE OVERWHELMING NUMBER OF RELEVANT RECORDS ARE THERE AND THIS IS THE LOCATION WHERE PLAINTIFF WOULD HAVE WORKED BUT FOR DEFENDANTS' ALLEGED WRONGFUL ACTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

Ntc And Mtn To Transfer Venue To Sacramento County
Off Calendar Sep-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CHARLES SCHWAB & CO., INC'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY is granted based on Government Code section 12965 (b). The parties agree that section 12965 (b) is the controlling venue statute. The declarations submitted by the defendants demonstrate that Sacramento County is the proper venue for this dispute. The decision to terminate plaintiff occurred there, the overwhelming number of relevant records are there and this is the location where plaintiff would have worked but for defendants' alleged wrongful acts. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
